mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by over 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns underperform the market baseline by more than 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects strengthening supply-side capacity that boosts short-term equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of observations when industrial production accelerates, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a static or rising federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a positive Rank IC exceeding 0.05, or 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation, high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no correlation with payroll growth trends.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 500,000 units signals weakening residential investment that reduces construction sector earnings and triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances when housing starts drop below 500,000 units, or the signal fails to predict direction during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy that prompts anticipatory risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in over 55% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge when the unemployment rate remains above 7.0%.
